problem_statement: Expected: the municipal permit portal exports a two-page records package to a temporary file and returns the completed PDF. Observed: the export process returns ENOSPC while its runtime reports substantial free byte capacity, including for a tiny diagnostic package. Diagnose the causal family without restarting or cleaning the live process.

observations.statement: A bounded process log records tempfile creation failing with ENOSPC before PDF rendering begins; the same error occurs for a 2 KB diagnostic payload and a normal two-page package.

A read-only filesystem stat from the affected container shows the temporary volume at 100 percent inode use with 68 percent of byte capacity free; the application volume and host volume both have free inodes.
The same signed image, source revision, settings, captured request, fonts, and dependency stubs complete the export in a fresh isolated container whose temporary volume has free inodes.
A font-substitution warning appears near the failures, but the tempfile error precedes font loading and the isolated container succeeds with the identical font directory hash.
The failing request fingerprint previously succeeded in another replica running the same image and settings, and the tiny diagnostic request rules out package size as the distinguishing condition.
